expectations.text: Mentions modal token new for first-time authentication
Mentions accepting the Llama license on HuggingFace before downloading (gated model)
Provides an HF_TOKEN setup step and creates an hf-token Modal secret
Pins transformers with an upper bound (e.g., <4.56) when installing vLLM
expectations.evidence:
matched: transformers>=4.51.1` and pip will happily pick 5.x which removed
#     methods that vllm still calls. cap at <4.5

expectations.text: Uses scaledown_window (or equivalent) NOT the deprecated container_idle_timeout
Stores model weights at a path containing the real org/name slash (e.g., /weights/meta-llama/Llama-...) NOT a sanitized __ version
Splits weight download (CPU container) from inference (GPU container) to avoid paying GPU rates for the download
Includes a runnable smoke test step that verifies one inference call works end-to-end
Identifies that Modal validates ALL secrets in the list at app-build time, not at function-call time
Provides a concrete fix: comment out / remove the unused secret line(s)
Shows the corrected code (with anthropic and google secrets removed or commented)
Does NOT suggest creating placeholder Anthropic secrets as the primary fix (a workaround, not a fix)
Diagnoses the cause as a transformers version mismatch (transformers 5.x missing the attribute that vLLM still calls)
Provides the fix: add an upper bound on transformers (e.g., transformers<4.56 or specific 4.x version)
Shows corrected pip_install code with the transformers pin
Explains WHY this happens (open upper bound in vllm's setup.py letting pip resolve to incompatible major) so the user can solve similar future issues
Does NOT recommend downgrading vLLM as the primary fix (the user is on a known-good vLLM; transformers is the actual problem)
Identifies that modal run (without --detach) ties app lifecycle to the local CLI, so client disconnect kills the app
Primary fix: re-launch with modal run --detach
Explains how to track progress for a detached run (Modal dashboard URL or modal app logs)
Addresses recovery: if the orchestrator wrote+committed to a volume per-trial, partial results survive and can be pulled with modal volume get
Mentions the per-trial flush + commit pattern as a prevention measure (resilience to preemption AND disconnect)
